question_title: Will a nuclear weapon be detonated in or above a European Union state before the following dates? (March 15, 2026)
summary: No nuclear weapon has been used in combat since 1945 (80+ years), and none has ever been detonated on EU territory. The resolution window is only ~35 days. While geopolitical tensions are elevated (Russia-Ukraine war, New START expiration on Feb 5, 2026), there is no credible scenario for nuclear detonation on EU soil in this timeframe. Multiple layers of deterrence remain intact. This is an extreme tail-risk event at the probability floor.

factors.description: 80+ year base rate of zero nuclear weapons use in combat since 1945
No nuclear weapon has ever been detonated on EU territory — zero historical precedent
Extremely short 35-day resolution window limits opportunity for escalation
Multiple deterrence mechanisms (NATO nuclear umbrella, MAD) remain intact
New START expired Feb 5 2026, arms control erosion increases long-term risk slightly
Russia-Ukraine conflict ongoing with occasional nuclear rhetoric

resolution_reason: The question asks whether a nuclear weapon will be detonated in or above the territory of an EU member state before March 15, 2026. Based on comprehensive web searches, there is no evidence of any nuclear weapon detonation in any EU member state. The Wikipedia article on "Nuclear warfare" explicitly confirms that "As of 2026, the first and only use of nuclear weapons in armed conflict was the United States atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, on August 6 and 9, 1945." While there have been significant geopolitical tensions (including the 2026 Iran war, which involved the Middle East rather than EU territory), no nuclear weapons have been detonated in Europe. The scheduled resolution date is March 15, 2026, and since no nuclear detonation has occurred in EU territory, this question resolves to NO.
resolution_criteria:
This question is a subquestion of a group question. This subquestion specifically pertains to (and resolves according to) the option 'March 15, 2026'. The resolution criteria for the parent question is below. 

This question will resolve **Yes** if a nuclear weapon is detonated in or above the territory of a European Union (EU) member state before the dates indicated.  A [deliberate, inadvertent, or accidental/unauthorised](http://scienceandglobalsecurity.org/archive/sgs21barrett.pdf) detonation would result in the question resolving as **Yes**.
